How to use lemongrass oil to keep lizards away: Easy tricks to stop these unwanted guests from climbing your walls
articles.description: Lemongrass oil offers a natural way to deter house lizards by masking their scent attractants and reducing insect prey. Its strong citrus aroma, particularly from compounds like citral, makes areas less appealing to lizards. Applying diluted lemongrass oil sprays or scented cotton balls near entry points and common lizard spots can help manage mild infestations.

The administration will take stern action against offenders.

articles.title: Quote of the Day by renowned physicist Stephen Hawking: 'Life would be tragic if...'- The greatest physicist's most powerful quote was not about science, rather about finding humor in human struggle
articles.description: Quote of the Day: Stephen Hawking, a renowned physicist, is remembered not only for his groundbreaking scientific contributions but also for his profound observation: 'Life would be tragic if it weren't funny.' Despite facing a devastating illness for decades, Hawking's intelligence, curiosity, and humor allowed him to endure unimaginable physical limitations, offering a powerful lesson in resilience and perspective.

The mortal remains were reportedly wrapped in the Tricolour as fans and well-wishers gathered to bid farewell.

articles.title: India-US trade surges elevenfold in two decades, crosses $220 billion mark: Sergio Gor
articles.description: At the Annual Leadership Summit of the American Chamber of Commerce, US Ambassador to India, Sergio Gor says, "... India’s expanding manufacturing ecosystem, digital infrastructure, innovation capabilities, and skilled talent pool complement America’s leadership in technology, investment, advanced research, and entrepreneurship. Our shared ambition of achieving $500 billion in bilateral trade by 2030 reflects the connectivity between our economies, the confidence built, and the scale of opportunity. We stand at a remarkable moment. Over the last two decades, bilateral trade has grown from about $20 billion to over $220 billion in goods and services — an elevenfold increase that reflects deep trust and strengthened economic integration. Today, the United States and India are among each other’s top trading partners... I am impressed by the individuals who visit the US Embassy each week. CEOs from the United States are here, choosing India over other parts of the world. Every week, top executives arrive — from Uber to Walmart, and recently Boeing, Lockheed, and GE. This is not a one off occurrence but a regular pattern, a testament to the limitless potential between our two countries."
